behavior_summary: U8P_NITSTAND::look is the object's U8P_NITSTAND::look/examine handler. When the player examines the nightstand it presents a single short on-screen label/bark reading "nightstand".
Immediately after displaying the label the process issues a suspend; this is inferred to terminate or yield the U8P_NITSTAND::look interaction so no further behavior runs in this handler.
There are no dialog menus, branching conditions, flag reads/writes, or external function calls recorded in this script.
notable_control_flow: U8P_NITSTAND::look executes linearly: emit Bark("nightstand") -> Suspend. No conditional branches or loops are present.
The handler is implemented as a process (process type 0x0208) and runs in response to an engine-driven U8P_NITSTAND::look/examine event.
open_questions: Does the 'suspend' call terminate the process permanently or merely yield/resume later in engine context? (inferred behavior only; confidence: low)
Are there any other related handlers for the same object (use, pickup, fast-area entry) not present in this excerpt that modify behavior? (confidence: low)
